title: Reporting a fork by macdirtycow which unlocks premium features

body:
Hey, I came across this fork of the project and thought it should be reported.
They basically force set the _isPremium boolean to true in their fork, which is just not okay in my opinion.

I'm not sure where to properly report this so I'm opening this issue so that you may do so yourself.

Here is the repository url:
https://github.com/macdirtycow/earthpods

title: [Linux] --start-minimized still shows "unknown" window in GNOME app switcher
author: villa1337
body:
## Description

When running LibrePods with `--start-minimized` on GNOME (Wayland), the app correctly starts with only the tray icon visible. However, an "unknown" window still appears in the GNOME app switcher (Alt+Tab).

## Environment

- **OS:** Fedora 44
- **DE:** GNOME (Wayland)
- **GPU:** NVIDIA RTX 5070 (proprietary driver 595.80)
- **LibrePods version:** v0.1.0 AppImage (linux-v0.1.0 release)
- **Launch command:** `WGPU_BACKEND=gl librepods-x86_64.AppImage --start-minimized`
- **Note:** `WGPU_BACKEND=gl` is required because Vulkan crashes with `ERROR_SURFACE_LOST_KHR` on this setup

## Expected Behavior

When started with `--start-minimized`, the app should only show the system tray icon with no window in the app switcher.

## Actual Behavior

A window labeled "unknown" appears in the GNOME app switcher. The tray icon works correctly.

## Possible Cause

The GPU rendering surface (wgpu/EGL window) is likely being created even in minimized mode. On GNOME Wayland, any mapped surface — even hidden — appears in the app switcher unless the appropriate window type hints are set or the surface is not created at all.

## Suggested Fix

In `--start-minimized` mode, either:
1. Don't create the GPU surface until the user explicitly opens the window from the tray
2. Set the window role/type so GNOME excludes it from the switcher
3. Unmap/destroy the surface after initialization if running headless

title: Proposal: cross-link as complementary projects (OpenHearing — hearing test + assist for any earbuds)
author: HMAKT99
body:
Hi! First — LibrePods is fantastic work, congrats on v1.0.

I maintain [OpenHearing](https://github.com/HMAKT99/OpenHearing) (GPLv3), which I think sits exactly next to LibrePods without overlapping:

- **LibrePods**: drives AirPods' own hearing-aid mode and features on Android (root, AirPods hardware).
- **OpenHearing**: does its own on-device pure-tone hearing test → per-ear amplification profile → real-time DSP assist. No root, works with any earbuds — but it can't talk to AirPods' native mode.

Your README explicitly (and reasonably) declines to build a hearing test; OpenHearing has one, and conversely we point people who own AirPods and want the native mode to LibrePods — our README already links to you, and your protocol docs are credited as the reference for our (not yet started) AirPods phase.

Would you be open to a short mutual mention — e.g. a line in your README's related-projects/FAQ area for people who arrive without AirPods or without root? Happy to send the PR so it's zero effort on your side, worded however you prefer. And if you'd rather not, totally fine — closing this is no offense taken.

Thanks for everything the project has documented — it's been genuinely useful.
— Arun (OpenHearing)

title: When installing on Pixel 8, it removes hearing aid setup from prior ipad setup
author: Nemi5150
body:
### App version

0.2.9-play

### App source

Play

### Device

Pixel 8

### Android / OS version

latest

### Root / hook method

No root (native L2CAP support)

### AirPods model

AirPods Pro 3

### AirPods firmware

NA

### What happened

I wrote a review and you wanted me to contact you, but I was not sure how to do that so here I am. 

I do not currently have the app installed. I have setup my APP3 on an ipad with ipados 26. I setup the hearing aid function inputting my audiogram from the doctor. Doing this and connecting to my pixel as a bluetooth device, I had no extended APP3 functionality, but it hearing aid function is stored on device and would modify the audio output even when connected to a non-apple device. 

after installing Librepods on my pixel and connecting the APP3, I gained the extended functionality that the app delivers, but immediately lost the hearing aid settings on the APP3. 

I then uninstalled Librepods and reconnected to the pixel and the hearing aid functionality was still missing. I then went back to the Ipad I originallly used to setup the APP3 and it "recognized" an available APP3 that could connect, as if it had never connected to them before. It then went through the initial setup on the ipad asking questions about what features I wanted setup. I skipped all and when I went into the settings for the APP3 on the ipad and selected the hearing aid feature to turn on, it allowed me to select the prior audiogram I had input before. All other settings that I have changed had to be reselected as if the APP3 had been reset. 

### Logs

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

title: Airpods Pro 2 and 3 disconnecting
author: AgentOfenkaese
body:
### App version

v1.0.0-rc1

### App source

GitHub

### Device

Nothing 3(a)

### Android / OS version

InfinityX (A16)

### Root / hook method

KernelSU + Xposed

### AirPods model

AirPods Pro 2 (USB-C)

### AirPods firmware

8454768

### What happened

When I connect to my AirPods (happens both on my Pro 2 and 3) and start playing media, they randomly start disconnecting shortly after. While the Pro 2 automatically reconnect almost instantly, the Pro 3 have trouble fully reconnecting, sometimes I need to put them back in my case in order to reconnect. If this happens they are connected according to Bluetooth settings, but arent able to play media. this started happening after updating to Librepods 1.0.0.

I'm using KernelSU Next + Vector from JingMatrix (API 101)
